fullArticle:
Welcome to Friday’s edition of the RaceFans round-up.

George Russell’s recent description of himself as being “working class” prompted some debate in the comments:

To be fair, even though I’m very much not a Russell fan, I would say he does indeed come from a working class family. His dad may have owned a business (which he sold to fund George’s racing career), but owning a business doesn’t automatically stop you being working class. Otherwise there wouldn’t be any working class tradesmen. Middle class was always defined as having professional qualifications, such as being a doctor or a teacher.
David (@Nvherman)
